behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels for attendee attribution.
behaviors.justification: The real failure mode is that the STT API either does not support speaker diarization, requires a specific request parameter to enable it, or returns labels in an unexpected schema. An in-process fake always returns whatever speaker labels we hardcode, so it cannot catch these failures. A real HTTP call to the STT API is required. REJECTION-GATE for Agentic: STT diarization is deterministic for the same audio input — repeated calls produce consistent speaker IDs — so LLM-style non-determinism does not apply.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a pre-recorded audio clip containing two or more distinct speakers to the real speech-to-text API and assert the returned transcript object contains a non-null speaker_label (or equivalent diarization field) on every utterance, covering all expected speaker IDs.

behaviors.description: Structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task.
behaviors.justification: The failure is the LLM omitting or misattributing action items from the transcript. A mocked LLM returns pre-programmed output, so it can never expose this failure — the test would pass regardless of LLM quality. Correctness here is inherently non-deterministic across LLM runs. REJECTION-GATE for Workflow: this behavior exercises a single summarization call; no multi-step agentic chain is required to catch the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a curated transcript containing explicit, named task assignments (e.g., 'Alice, please send the report by Friday; Bob, schedule the vendor call') to the live LLM summarizer and assert that action_items in the returned structured output contains at least one item attributed to each named assignee.

behaviors.description: Key decisions appear in the 'decisions' section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: The failure is the LLM categorizing decision content into the narrative field rather than the decisions field. A mocked LLM would produce whatever structure we hardcode, making the test trivially pass regardless of LLM behavior. The categorization judgment is inherently LLM-dependent and can vary across runs. REJECTION-GATE for Workflow: only a single LLM summarization call is exercised; no sequential multi-step chain is needed to observe the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript containing several clearly-worded decisions (e.g., 'We decided to launch in Q3', 'The team agreed to switch vendors') to the real LLM summarizer and assert that those decision statements appear in the decisions field of the structured output, while the narrative field does not contain equivalent text.

behaviors.description: Audio longer than 60 minutes is split into segments, processed sequentially, and merged into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires: (a) the segmentation boundary to trigger, (b) each segment to be independently transcribed and summarized via real LLM calls, and (c) the merge step to produce a coherent unified summary. The coherence claim across segments can only be verified with real LLM calls, and the full sequence constitutes multiple agentic steps in sequence. REJECTION-GATE for Agentic (single step): a single Agentic test covers one LLM call; it cannot verify cross-segment coherence or that the sequential-merge pipeline produces consistent output end-to-end. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: structural merging (field concatenation) can be tested at Integration, but the 'single coherent summary' quality claim cannot.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a >60-minute audio recording (or a synthetic fixture that triggers the segment threshold) through the full pipeline with real LLM calls enabled. Assert that: (a) the service emits log/event evidence of multiple segment calls, (b) each segment produces a partial structured summary, and (c) the final merged output is a single structured document containing action items and decisions drawn from content present in all segments, with no duplicate or contradictory entries.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an explicit end-to-end pipeline test. Every stage requires real infrastructure: real audio upload (OS/network I/O), real STT HTTP API call, real LLM summarization call, and real structured output delivery. The behavior specifically requires a real meeting recording and autonomous completion. No single lower level can expose failures at the handoff boundaries between pipeline stages. MULTI-STEP: upload → transcribe (STT) → summarize (LLM) → deliver is a multi-step agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Upload a real meeting audio recording to the service endpoint, poll or subscribe for completion without any manual step, and assert the delivered structured output: (a) has non-empty action_items list, (b) has non-empty decisions list, (c) has attendee attributions linking statements to real speaker names from the recording, and (d) the entire run completes within a defined SLA timeout with no human-in-the-loop prompts.
